frame: Legal-status layer sets the tax floor (ITIN/undocumented payments) and the population stock (Pew residual) feeding lifetime NPV. Border enforcement shifts Mexican immigrant selection (Lozano-López) — earnings path, not just headcount.

parameter_claims.notes: Federal $59.4B + state/local $37.3B. Unnamed: annual flow ≈ lifetime NPV tax floor without mortality/discount bridge.
$8.9B per 1M population. Microsim on Pew/CMS stock — not independently observed payments.

parameter_claims.page_ref: p.3 Key Findings; p.6 Figure 2 ($33.9B of $96.7B)
parameter_claims.notes: SS $25.7B + Medicare $6.4B + UI $1.8B. Workers barred from most program benefits — pure fiscal transfer to PAYG systems.

parameter_claims.population: undocumented immigrants vs median U.S. income group
parameter_claims.notes: 26.1% vs 26.4% median. Unnamed: effective rate parity implies similar federal proxy once income matched — ignores benefit ineligibility wedge.

parameter_claims.population: undocumented tax units, federal individual income tax
parameter_claims.notes: Midpoint of 50–75% CBO literature band; sensitivity ±15.5% on grand total per json. Unnamed: contribution rate = compliance for lifetime scenarios.

parameter_claims.population: scenario: all current undocumented granted work authorization
parameter_claims.notes: Total rises to $136.9B (+$33.1B federal, +$7.1B state/local). Unnamed: legalization uplift applied linearly over remaining lifetime.
Record high; +3.5M in two years (2021–2023). Supersedes prior Pew estimates. Stock input for fiscal models — 2.5M gap vs ITEP 10.9M (2022) reflects timing + definition.
~6M of 14M with parole/asylum/TPS-style protection. Unnamed: treated as unauthorized for tax but may differ in work authorization and benefit access — fiscal status heterogeneous.
6.4M → 9.7M; Mexico flat ~4.3M. Unnamed: Mexico-weighted lifetime cells understate recent-surge composition shift (Venezuela, Cuba, Haiti parole paths).
5.6% of workforce vs 4.1% of total population — age composition skew. ITEP reports 4.7% workforce / 3.4% population on 2022 stock.
Up from $400M (1992). Context for rising coyote costs and female-biased crossing mortality (9%→21% of deaths 1998–2005).

parameter_claims.population: Mexican immigrants, post-2000 vs 1984 cohort, triple-diff vs other Latin America
parameter_claims.notes: Diff-in-diff female share −12.2pp (p<0.001); earnings diff-in-diff +10.4–12.5% for selected groups. Unnamed: 2000s selection parameters apply to 2020s Mexico surge.

generators.name: Tax-floor vs benefit-ban ledger split
generators.prompt: For any undocumented/refugee fiscal cell, decompose annual flows into (a) taxes paid regardless of status — payroll, sales, property pass-through, ITIN income tax — and (b) benefits legally barred. ITEP shows 35% of taxes fund SS/Medicare/UI with exclusion from benefits. Do not net to zero without explicit benefit-access matrix.
generators.retrodiction: Would have separated ITEP $96.7B tax floor from NAS benefit-cost rows before treating unauthorized as net-negative by default.
generators.negative_space: Single net fiscal scalar assuming undocumented immigrants receive Medicaid/SNAP at citizen rates.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Tax payments require legal status to count toward lifetime NPV
Payroll taxes without benefit eligibility are fiscally neutral
generators.theory_tested: Undocumented immigrants pay meaningful taxes while barred from major benefits

generators.name: Legal-status stock reconciler
generators.prompt: Before lifetime NPV, reconcile population stocks: Pew residual (14M 2023, upward undercount adjustment) vs ITEP/Warren (10.9M 2022) vs temp-protection subgroups (6M). Map each stock to work-authorization, ITIN filing, and benefit-eligibility states. Flag when model uses one stock for taxes and another for costs.
generators.retrodiction: Would have caught 2.5M headcount mismatch and 40% temp-protection heterogeneity before applying scalar Mexico undocumented parameters.
generators.negative_space: Single undocumented count from one source year applied to all fiscal lines.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Unauthorized = undocumented = same benefit and tax treatment
Parolees/asylum seekers follow ITEP undocumented tax parameters
generators.theory_tested: Legal-status heterogeneity within unauthorized stock drives tax vs transfer mapping

generators.name: ITIN compliance sensitivity band
generators.prompt: Treat federal income tax contribution rate as explicit sensitivity: ITEP base 60% (50–75% literature), json pessimistic −10.6% / optimistic +15.5% on grand total. Require any lifetime undocumented cell to show tax floor under {50%, 60%, 75%} before point estimate.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced ITEP advocacy-adjacent microsim and CBO 50–75% band before locking undocumented federal income proxy.
generators.negative_space: 100% compliance or 0% undocumented income tax as unstated extremes.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: 60% contribution rate is representative across states and origins
Over-withholding non-filers net to zero lifetime fiscal effect
generators.theory_tested: Undocumented tax floor is robust to ±15% contribution-rate perturbation

generators.name: Border-selection earnings updater
generators.prompt: When calibrating Mexico-origin lifetime cells, ask whether border-enforcement era shifted immigrant selection (Lozano-López: fewer older/high-ed women, higher earnings among survivors). Compare pre-2000 vs post-2000 arrival cohorts; do not use pooled Mexico <HS wage path for recent-surge entrants without selection flag.
generators.retrodiction: Would have linked IZA dp4898 positive selection channel to Mexico earnings donor cells before assuming uniform undocumented wage floor.
generators.negative_space: Random emigration from Mexican wage distribution (no gender/cost selection).
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Border enforcement selection estimated on 1990–2008 cohorts applies to 2021–2023 surge
Higher selected earnings imply higher lifetime NPV without tenure offset
generators.theory_tested: Rising migration costs positively select Mexican immigrant women on observables

generators.name: Tenure-split forward stock
generators.prompt: Pew 2023: 3.2M unauthorized <5 years vs 4.3M 18+ years. Split lifetime NPV into forward stock (short tenure, high flow uncertainty, temp protection) vs established stock (ITEP 16+ year median). Apply different discount horizons and legalization uplift ($40.2B/yr scenario) by tenure bucket.
generators.retrodiction: Would have prevented applying established-immigrant tax parameters to 2021–2023 parole-heavy inflow before forward-stock analysis.
generators.negative_space: Steady-state unauthorized population with homogeneous tenure.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Average tenure equals marginal entrant tenure
Short-tenure unauthorized have same per-capita tax as 16+ year residents
generators.theory_tested: Tenure heterogeneity splits undocumented lifetime NPV into distinct forward vs established stocks

theories_tested.theory: Undocumented tax floor (~$8.9k/yr cap-weighted) offsets fiscal cost even with benefit exclusion
theories_tested.prediction: Applying ITEP per-capita tax × Pew stock − barred-benefit transfers yields positive federal/state flow for working-age unauthorized cells before Medicaid retirement phase
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Compose scenario: pew_unauthorized_stock × itep_per_capita_tax by state; subtract NAS benefit rows scaled to documented-ineligible matrix; compare to zero under SSA 3% discount
theories_tested.falsifier: Barred-benefit savings exceed ITEP tax floor for Mexico-weighted <HS unauthorized cell at 2023 stock levels

theories_tested.theory: Border enforcement positively selects Mexican immigrant earnings (especially women)
theories_tested.prediction: Post-2000 Mexico female arrivals show higher ACS wage percentiles than pre-1990 cohort controlling for education — consistent with Lozano-López +10% earnings diff-in-diff
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Split acs_origin_household by arrival_era {pre-2000, post-2000}; compare female Mexico wage donor to male and to non-Mexico Latin America placebo
theories_tested.falsifier: No earnings premium on post-2000 female Mexico cohort; selection parameters indistinguishable from zero

theories_tested.theory: Recent-surge unauthorized (short tenure + temp protection) carry lower lifetime forward stock than established cohort
theories_tested.prediction: Pew <5yr bucket (3.2M) has lower NPV than 18+yr bucket (4.3M) when temp-protection revocation risk and incomplete labor attachment priced in
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Two-scenario compose: tenure<5 vs tenure18+ × {ITEP base tax, legalization uplift}; apply higher discount or exit hazard to temp-protection 40% subshare
theories_tested.falsifier: Tenure buckets produce identical lifetime NPV under reasonable exit/revocation bands
